differential_diagnoses: Systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE) with lupus nephritis and secondary antiphospholipid syndrome
Primary antiphospholipid syndrome (APS) with catastrophic APS presentation
Membranous nephropathy complicated by renal vein thrombosis and pulmonary thromboembolism
Renal cell carcinoma with tumor thrombus and paraneoplastic hypercoagulability
next_steps: Perform diagnostic serology including ANA, dsDNA, complement levels (C3, C4), and antiphospholipid antibody panel (lupus anticoagulant, anticardiolipin, anti-beta2 glycoprotein I)
Initiate therapeutic anticoagulation (e.g., heparin drip) given confirmed bilateral renal vein thrombosis and pulmonary embolism
Consider ultrasound-guided renal biopsy to determine the etiology of nephrotic-range proteinuria and hematuria
Consult rheumatology and hematology for management of potential autoimmune disease and hypercoagulable state
Assess for signs of catastrophic antiphospholipid syndrome (CAPS) which requires aggressive multidisciplinary management
Continue broad-spectrum antibiotics pending blood and urine culture results to rule out urosepsis

differential_diagnoses: Systemic Lupus Erythematosus with lupus nephritis and secondary antiphospholipid syndrome
Primary Antiphospholipid Syndrome presenting as Catastrophic APS
Membranous nephropathy complicated by renal vein thrombosis and pulmonary thromboembolism
Infective endocarditis with septic pulmonary emboli and renal infarction
next_steps: Perform comprehensive autoimmune serology (ANA, dsDNA, complement C3/C4, anti-PLA2R, anti-GBM, and antiphospholipid antibody panel)
Initiate therapeutic anticoagulation with intravenous heparin
Obtain an echocardiogram to evaluate for cardiac sources of emboli and vegetations
Schedule an urgent renal biopsy once the patient is hemodynamically stable and anticoagulation is managed
Obtain formal infectious disease consultation and send blood and urine cultures (pre-antibiotic if possible) to rule out occult infection
Perform serum and urine protein electrophoresis to screen for monoclonal gammopathy
Review family history for genetic kidney disease, specifically screening for complement-related disorders or rare nephropathies
